page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating on the ARM64 architecture, this instance features 4 dedicated physical cores with 4 vCPUs and a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB. Local storage is provided via a 237 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across several standard compute and memory operations. The instance achieves average ratings in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as in Geekbench single-core and multi-core evaluations. It demonstrates strong single-threaded performance in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, the instance exhibits performance limitations in specific areas, scoring in the bottom 25% for Passmark CPU and extended instructions tests, and in the bottom 10% for the Passmark CPU encryption test. LLM inference speeds for text generation and prompt processing are also positioned in the bottom 25%.

From a resource-density perspective, the c8gd.xlarge offers a balanced profile for workloads requiring dedicated ARM64 cores and fast local NVMe storage without the overhead of hyperthreading. The primary hardware tradeoff is the low memory-to-CPU ratio of 2 GB per core, which makes it less suitable for memory-intensive applications. Given its average database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput, this instance is well-suited for web serving, software compilation, lightweight database hosting, and general-purpose ARM64 development.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance powered by a 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores (4 vCPUs), 8.0 GB of memory, and 237 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, running on the Nitro hypervisor with a 2 Gbps baseline network. Benchmarks show average performance in general compute, software compilation, and database operations, alongside strong single-threaded compression capabilities. However, it exhibits weak performance in encryption and LLM inference. With a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio, this instance represents a specialized hardware profile for workloads that benefit from dedicated ARM cores and local high-speed storage. It is best suited for static web serving, software compilation, and lightweight database applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 8.0 GB RAM, and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD, built for web serving and compilation.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated Graviton cores, 8 GB RAM, and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and local NVMe storage for efficient web serving and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores.
Equipped with 8.0 GB of memory and 237 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Strong single-threaded performance in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression.
Exhibits lower performance in encryption and LLM inference workloads.
Optimized for static web serving, software compilation, and lightweight databases.
